# Classical Track - Metrics Tables

*Generated by `scripts/make_report_figures.py` from committed run artifacts (no invented numbers). Genuine = held-out La Salle test (56 probes); impostors = LFW (13,149 Haar-cropped); 41-modification AR on the same held-out originals. Operating point = spec FAR budget (100 ppm; actual 76 ppm, the LFW-resolvable floor).*

---

## Table 1 - Main Performance Summary

| Algorithm | TAR % | FAR (ppm) | FRR % | Mean AR % (41 mods) | Latency (ms) | Model size (KB) | Feature (bytes) | Pass/Fail vs spec |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|:--|
| **Spec target** | 90-95 | < 100 | 1-5 | (robustness) | < 100 | (Pi context) | < 1024 | - |
| LBPH | 98.21 | 76 | 1.79 | 85.43 | 50.2 | 32,474 | 65,536 | **PASS** (accuracy block); feature >1 KB, latency OK |
| Eigenfaces | 23.21 | 76 | 76.79 | 47.69 | 34.4 | 80,630 | 1,120 | **FAIL** (TAR/FRR); feature >1 KB, latency OK |
| Fisherfaces | 10.71 | 76 | 89.29 | 30.54 | 36.0 | 7,993 | 108 | **FAIL** (TAR/FRR); feature <1 KB OK, latency OK |

TAR/FAR/FRR are verification operating points on each recognizer's native `predict()` scale at the 100 ppm spec FAR budget (`tar_at_far.json`). Latency = 1000 / measured end-to-end live FPS (detection + recognition; the recognizer `predict()` itself is sub-ms - the Haar detector dominates, and the LBPH FPS log predates the Tan-Triggs switch). Model size is the saved `.yml`; feature size is per-face (LBPH 8x8x256 hist; Eigen/Fisher comps x 4 B).

---

## Table 2 - Accuracy Ratio by modification category

AR = K/M (correct identity AND within the deployable threshold) per (modification, level); the mean over a modification's levels is its AR. Levels are bucketed into severity tiers - **Light** (mildest) / **Medium** / **Heavy** (most severe) - by perturbation magnitude; symmetric levels (e.g. rotation -5deg & +5deg) share a tier, so rotation/zoom have an empty Medium. `Mean` is the all-level mean (the headline per-modification AR).

### 2a - Merged comparison (mean AR % per modification)

| Modification | LBPH | Eigenfaces | Fisherfaces |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| occlusion | 98.8 | 53.6 | 32.7 |
| gamma_up | 98.2 | 53.6 | 35.7 |
| contrast_down | 98.2 | 55.4 | 35.7 |
| brightness_up | 97.8 | 53.6 | 35.7 |
| gamma_down | 97.6 | 51.8 | 33.3 |
| gaussian_blur | 88.1 | 51.2 | 36.3 |
| zoom | 87.9 | 41.5 | 27.2 |
| contrast_up | 85.1 | 44.0 | 26.2 |
| rotation | 83.5 | 26.3 | 14.3 |
| brightness_down | 73.7 | 38.4 | 20.1 |
| motion_blur | 68.5 | 51.2 | 35.7 |
| gaussian_noise | 47.8 | 51.8 | 33.5 |
| **Overall (mean of mods)** | **85.4** | **47.7** | **30.5** |

### 2b - Fisherfaces: AR % by severity tier

| Modification | Light | Medium | Heavy | Mean |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| occlusion | 33.9 | 32.1 | 32.1 | 32.7 |
| gamma_up | 35.7 | 35.7 | 35.7 | 35.7 |

| rotation | 22.3 | - | 6.2 | 14.3 |
| brightness_down | 32.1 | 21.4 | 5.4 | 20.1 |
| motion_blur | 33.9 | 35.7 | 37.5 | 35.7 |
| gaussian_noise | 33.9 | 33.9 | 32.1 | 33.5 |
| **Overall** | | | | **30.5** |

> Geometric distortion is the classical failure mode: **rotation** is the single weakest modification for Eigenfaces (26.3 %) and Fisherfaces (14.3 %). The suite has no pure translation/crop modification - rotation and zoom (scale) are the geometric proxies present; LBPH absorbs them (83 % / 88 %) far better than the subspace methods.

---

## Table 3 - Independence Test Results

Threshold = feature distance of the k-th nearest cross-identity (impostor) pair at the target FAR (spec rank rule). Scales are per-model and per-dataset (not comparable across rows). La Salle and LFW use different feature bases.

| Algorithm | Dataset | Comparison set size | FAR target (ppm) | Realized FAR (ppm) | Threshold (raw) | Threshold (norm 0-100) | Boundary pair | Status |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|:--|:--|
| LBPH | La Salle DB1 | 756 (28x27) | 10,000 (8th pair) | 10,582 | 21.35 | 85.88 | Kenlie_John_Maghari vs Francis_Cromwell_Tan | Determined (1.06% FAR floor) |
| Eigenfaces | La Salle DB1 | 756 (28x27) | 10,000 (8th pair) | 10,582 | 8098.46 | 71.00 | Michael_Andrei_Jugado vs Francis_Miles_Ang | Determined (1.06% FAR floor) |
| Fisherfaces | La Salle DB1 | 756 (28x27) | 10,000 (8th pair) | 10,582 | 5446.46 | 66.38 | Francis_Cromwell_Tan vs Anthony_Charles_Saltat | Determined (1.06% FAR floor) |
| LBPH | LFW DB1 (smoke) | 89,700 (300 ids) | 10 (331st pair) | 11.1 | 12.76 | 38.63 | Andrew_Caldecott vs Andrew_Gilligan | Smoke; full 33M run pending |
| Eigenfaces | LFW DB1 (smoke) | 89,700 (300 ids) | 10 (331st pair) | 11.1 | 917.81 | 7.26 | Andrew_Caldecott vs Andrew_Gilligan | Smoke; full 33M run pending |
| Fisherfaces | LFW DB1 (smoke) | 89,700 (300 ids) | 10 (331st pair) | 11.1 | 897.91 | 15.22 | Andrew_Caldecott vs Andrew_Gilligan | Smoke; full 33M run pending |

- **La Salle DB1** (756 = 28x27 comparisons) determines each model's threshold at its design operating point (8th error pair = 10,582 ppm = 1.058 %). 756 comparisons cannot resolve the spec's 100 ppm / 10 ppm - that needs LFW DB1.
- **LFW DB1** is the gated full protocol (5,749 ids -> 33,045,252 comparisons, 331st pair ~ 10 ppm). The committed run is a **300-identity smoke** (89,700 comparisons, k=1 ~ 11 ppm); all three families flag the **same boundary pair (Andrew_Caldecott vs Andrew_Gilligan)** - the known LFW annotation-error failure mode to eyeball before trusting full-run thresholds.
- 'Pass/Fail' for the independence test is whether the target FAR is *resolvable*: La Salle resolves only ~1,300 ppm (so 100 ppm is a FAIL on La Salle alone, PASS as a 1.058 % operating point); the LFW full run is the protocol-grade certification and is **pending**.
